title: Move to v3.1.0-dev
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

You’re targeting master - need to make a branch for this. Also, should be
3.0.0-rc1 not 3.0.0.

On Sun, Jan 17, 2021 at 15:52 OpenShift CI Robot <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> [APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is *APPROVED*
>
> This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan

> .
>
The other PR is for v3.0 branch.  Your description did not state that you want the -rc1 flag on it.  I will update the other Branch.

Master should not bump to 3.0 - it should just go to 3.1.0-dev. The 3.0.0
and its RCs live only in the branch

On Mon, Jan 18, 2021 at 06:52 Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> The other PR is for v3.0 branch. Your description did not state that you
> want the -rc1 flag on it. I will update the other Branch.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you commented.
>
>
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8998#issuecomment-762200557>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCGRY3Y63IFLDY5BL4TS2QOI5ANCNFSM4WGMSSCA>
> .
>
@mheon I want to have the 3.0.0 changes in the main branch?
No, we do not want the 3.0 commit to be in two branches simultaneously. 3.0 is only on the branch, master bumps to the next minor version.
Ok you add the commit the way that you want.  I would think we should have release_notes for 3.0 on main branch.
Release notes, yep, but no "bump to 3.0" commit - will just do a CI:DOCS to port only the release notes over.
